data.description: Deferred hardening (accepted limitation, ADR 0046). The commit-ancestry key-validity model (attest/authorship.py verify_authorship_at_commit) defeats the direct revoked-key-reuse case (a new event descends the revocation commit, so a revoked key's revoke-commit is an ancestor and the key is excluded). Its integrity rests on the tickets-branch commit graph being append-only, which today is a CONTROL (Gerrit-authenticated, controlled-environment write access), not a cryptographic guarantee. gittuf shipped the same log-position model and still needed a monotonic policy counter (CVE-2026-44544); TUF hard-codes N+1 root versioning. Future hardening: (a) a regression test asserting a revoked-then-re-introduced key at a LATER ancestry position FAILS; (b) documented threat analysis of tickets-branch force-push/rewrite and the controls that bound it; (c) evaluate an explicit monotonic anti-rollback guard as defense-in-depth. Not scheduled.
data.title: Cryptographic anti-rollback for commit-ancestry key validity (monotonic guard + regression test + tickets-branch-rewrite analysis)
